frame_analyses.description: Title frame: 'Laparoscopic tubal ligation' — instructional content label visible
Anatomical overview diagram showing pelvis with fallopian tubes
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional context — not a performance task

frame_analyses.description: Anatomical diagram of pelvis without markings
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational layout — no instruments or task initiation

frame_analyses.description: Anatomical diagram with red marking on left ovary/structure
Laparoscopic view with Penrose drain (pink) and Endoloop applicator (blue/purple) visible at port
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment introduction frame — timing begins here per rubric

frame_analyses.description: Endoloop applicator being advanced through port toward Penrose drain target
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth instrument insertion; drain in field

frame_analyses.description: Endoloop applicator positioned in close proximity to Penrose drain
Loop being opened and maneuvered around the Penrose drain structure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled positioning; loop expands as pusher retracts

frame_analyses.description: Loop fully opened around the drain; applicator tip visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop encompasses drain tissue; ready for advancement

frame_analyses.description: Ligating loop cinched and advanced around the drain using pusher mechanism
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot tightening visible; loop secured at base of structure

frame_analyses.description: Loop fully cinched around drain; pusher still engaged
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Secure knot placement at marked site; tension appears appropriate

frame_analyses.description: Scissors introduced to cut the loop tail material inside the trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final phase — cutting distal end of loop material

frame_analyses.description: Loop tail severed with endoscopic scissors
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion — loop secured and tail removed

frame_analyses.description: Final anatomy view showing ligated Fallopian tube (black loop visible around tissue)
Title frame: 'Mini-laparotomy Tubal Ligation' — transition to different procedure

video_classification_rationale:
This is INSTRUCTIONAL content. The video contains:
1. Educational title cards ('Laparoscopic tubal ligation', 'Mini-laparotomy Tubal Ligation')
2. Anatomical diagrams with educational overlays
3. Labeled equipment and target anatomy
4. Demonstration of correct technique without performance timing context
5. Transition to alternative procedure (mini-laparotomy)
6. No visible trainee performing under time pressure or evaluation conditions
7. Nucleus Medical Media copyright — professional educational material

Per the rubric: instructional videos with educational framing are NOT scored; teaching content is extracted instead.
cannot_score_reason: Video is instructional/educational material, not a trainee performance under FLS evaluation conditions. Per assessment instructions: 'instructional — Educational/tutorial content with narration, overlays, or lecture (do NOT score; extract teaching content only)'.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper introduction of endoloop applicator through laparoscopic port
Opening and positioning the ligating loop around target anatomy (Penrose drain surrogate)
Controlled advancement of pusher to cinch knot at marked site
Proper tension application to secure ligating loop without excessive compression
Cutting of loop tail inside the trainer using endoscopic scissors
Loop placement on Fallopian tube with successful ligation result
verbal_cues: Title: 'Laparoscopic tubal ligation' — establishes procedure context
Anatomical diagrams with target marking — shows mark location on structure
Progression through phases with clear sequence of steps
common_errors_addressed: Proper loop positioning before cinching (avoids premature knot formation)
Controlled pusher advancement (avoids excessive tissue compression or knot slippage)
Complete tail cut inside trainer (avoids incomplete task)
Loop placement at marked anatomical site (demonstrates precision requirement)
teaching_content.procedural_context: Laparoscopic tubal ligation using pre-tied endoloop ligating device; demonstrates standard technique for fallopian tube occlusion in gynecological procedures
equipment_shown: Penrose drain (tissue surrogate in FLS context; actual fallopian tube in clinical context)

note: This video serves as an expert reference demonstration for endoloop technique. It is suitable for trainee review prior to performing FLS Task 3 or as a teaching resource for procedural understanding. No performance score is applicable.
